item.title: 6/21: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Lee Cowan. Featured: Birthright citizenship; the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library unveiled; Shooter Jennings releases recordings by his father, Waylon Jennings; comedian John Mulaney; childhood obesity; and the secrets of seahorses and seadragons.

item.title: Man falls to death during Goose concert at MSG, NYPD says
item.description: A concertgoer fell to his death during rock band Goose's performance Saturday at Madison Square Garden, the New York Police Department said.

item.title: Pilot reports passenger bit fellow flyer on plane approaching Philadelphia
item.description: An American Airlines passenger allegedly bit a fellow flyer and was "trying to fight everybody" on a Sunday flight, a pilot said.

item.title: Rep. Jason Crow says he's worried "Americans are at risk" with Pulte as acting DNI
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado said he's worried that "Americans are at risk" with Bill Pulte serving as the top intelligence chief.

item.title: U.S., Iranian negotiators meet; Trump threatens Iran over Hezbollah
item.description: The appearance was a rare face-to-face meeting between U.S. and Iranian officials as they launched a 60-day sprint to negotiate over the fate of Iran's nuclear program.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 21)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Trump administration "laser focused" on Iran's nuclear program, Waltz says
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz said the Trump administration is "laser focused" on addressing Iran's nuclear program in an interview on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

item.title: 6/21: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S. and Iran launch delicate diplomatic talks, U.N. Ambassador Mike Waltz joins, along with GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham and Democratic Rep. Jason Crow.

item.title: Face the Nation: Crow, Book, Hochstein, Salvanto
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? Democratic Rep. Jason Crow discusses national security, and Kevin Book and Amos Hochstein break down the U.S.-Iran deal's impact on energy. Plus, CBS News' Anthony Salvanto discusses new polling on the Iran war.

item.title: Transcript: Lindsey Graham on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 21, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 21, 2026.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham: "Let's try a diplomatic solution. I think it's going to fail."
item.description: GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina said he expects a diplomatic solution with Iran to fail, though he noted that he would "rather try diplomacy than take it off the table."

item.title: Returning to pre-Iran war gas prices could take awhile, energy analyst says
item.description: On "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," energy analyst Kevin Book says it could take "a while" for gas prices to return to pre-Iran war levels, pointing to uncertainty and depleted oil reserves. And Biden-era adviser Amos Hochstein discusses how the U.S.-Iran deal could impact Iranian oil revenue.

item.title: CBS News poll finds most Americans want Iran war to end but don't think U.S. got better end of deal
item.description: CBS News Executive Director of Elections and Surveys Anthony Salvanto breaks down new polling on the Iran war, which most Americans wanted to end but few believe was worth the cost, on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

item.title: Obama urges resiliency and unity during speech at Obama Presidential Center opening
item.description: Former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama were joined by their daughters Thursday at the opening ceremony for the Obama Presidential Center on the South Side of Chicago, one day before it officially opened to the public on Juneteenth.

item.title: Transcript: Lindsey Graham on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 21, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 21, 2026.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham on Iran talks: "Let's try a diplomatic solution. I think it's going to fail"
item.description: On "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham said he supports trying diplomacy with Iran, but "I think it's going to fail," predicting President Trump's backup plan will be to take over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rep. Jason Crow worried acting intel chief Pulte is "incompetent" and a "political attack dog"
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jason Crow said on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" he is concerned acting Director of National Intelligence Bill Pulte is "incompetent" and a "political attack dog." Crow also said he's "unwilling to trade Americans' constitutional rights" to extend a key FISA spying power.

item.title: Transcript: Mike Waltz on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 21, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Mike Waltz, U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 21, 2026.

item.title: Mike Waltz: Iranian officials aren't "good guys," but Trump "laser focused" on nuclear weapons
item.description: As delicate U.S.-Iran talks kick off in Switzerland, U.N. Ambassador Mike Waltz told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" the Trump administration understands Iranian officials aren't "good guys," but the U.S. is taking a "pragmatic approach" focused on reaching a nuclear accord.

item.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 21, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," a thorny set of U.S.-Iran talks have begun amid threats surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing strikes between Israel and Hezbollah. U.N Ambassador Mike Waltz, GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham and Democratic Rep. Jason Crow discuss the talks.

item.title: Nature: John Muir Wilderness in California
item.description: We leave you this first of Sunday of summer at the John Muir Wilderness in California's Sierra Nevadas. Videographer: Scot Miller.

item.title: Charles M. Blow praises men who step up
item.description: The political commentator talks about the need to mentor young boys whose fathers are absent or passed, and how the organization Son of a Saint helps transform the lives of fatherless boys.

item.title: Charles M. Blow: In praise of men who step up
item.description: The political commentator talks about the need to mentor young boys whose fathers are absent or passed, and about the New Orleans youth organization Son of a Saint, which provides the guidance and correction, the modeling of composure, and the sense of possibility that will help transform the lives of fatherless boys.

item.title: Newsom declares state of emergency in Los Angeles for Boyle Heights warehouse fire
item.description: Smoke continues to billow from a large Boyle Heights cold storage facility on Sunday, where firefighting conditions remain highly complex, as Gov. Gavin Newsom and Mayor Karen Bass have declared a local emergency to support response efforts.

item.title: Fighting childhood obesity, for a healthier, longer life
item.description: In 1970, about 1 in 20 children were affected by obesity; today, it's 1 in 5. Dr. Jonathan LaPook looks at programs aimed at helping kids (and their families) get healthy the old-fashioned way, by eating right and exercising.

item.title: Seahorses and seadragons, some of Nature's most curious creatures
item.description: Seahorses are unique ocean inhabitants with a head like a horse, a pouch like a kangaroo, a tail like a monkey, and the ability to camouflage themselves like a chameleon. They also exhibit an unconventional gender dynamic, in that the males do the work of carrying around fertilized eggs. Correspondent Conor Knighton goes in search of these fascinating fish – and their equally fascinating cousins, seadragons – at the Birch Aquarium at the Scripps Institution of Oceanography in California.

item.title: Born in the U.S.A.: Protecting the right of birthright citizenship
item.description: As guaranteed in the 14th Amendment, citizenship is granted to "all persons born or naturalized in the United States." But an executive order signed by President Trump seeks to deny birthright citizenship to children born of parents in the country illegally or temporarily.

item.title: Poll finds Americans want Iran war ended, but few say U.S. got better of agreement
item.description: Most suspect Iran nuclear program not stopped and think conflict wasn't worth the costs.

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: States not required to give community-based care for those with disabilities: DOJ opinion
item.description: The Office of Legal Counsel opinion released Thursday said states aren't required by law to integrate mentally disabled patients with their peers by providing community or home-based care.
